event_name: Marietta History Center Family Fun: Rockets and Robots
venue: Marietta History Center, 1 Depot St., Marietta
blurb: The Marietta History Center is doing its monthly Pop-in for Family Fun this Saturday, and this month's theme is Rockets and Robots. Free admission, arts and crafts, and a few hours of hands-on fun for the kids from 11am to 2pm. It is right in Marietta Square, so you can grab lunch before or after and make a morning of it. No tickets, no hassle. Just show up.
scoring_notes: Recurring monthly event, which hurts the uniqueness score significantly. That said, East Cobb still has a meaningful segment of younger families and the location is extremely convenient. Free admission removes all friction. Works well as a secondary feature or calendar item but is not strong enough to be the top featured event on its own.

blurb: The Powder Springs Seafood Festival runs all weekend at Thurman Springs Park, just a short drive from East Cobb. Three days of seafood, artisan vendors, live entertainment, and outdoor family fun. If you are looking for something easy to do Saturday or Sunday with good food and no real agenda, this fits the bill. Check the schedule before you go so you catch the live music.
scoring_notes: Seafood festival is a natural fit for an established, foodie-leaning suburban demographic. Three-day run gives flexibility. Close enough to East Cobb to be worth including. Not a marquee event but a solid option for readers who want a low-pressure outdoor outing. Uniqueness is moderate since food festivals are common in this corridor.

event_name: Beyond Juicery + Eatery East Cobb Grand Opening
blurb: East Cobb is getting a new spot this weekend. Beyond Juicery + Eatery opens its East Cobb doors on May 16 with free drinks, prizes, and a chance to win smoothies for a year. If you have been curious about the brand, this is the cheapest possible way to try it. Stop in, grab something free, and see if it earns a regular spot in your routine.
scoring_notes: Health-forward food concept fits well with East Cobb's educated, higher-income demographic. Grand openings are inherently one-time events, which bumps the uniqueness score. The free offer and local angle make it an easy inclusion. Not a blockbuster featured event, but solid as a secondary calendar item or filler. Main weakness is it reads more like a marketing event than a community event.

blurb: Virginia Highland Porchfest is one of those Atlanta events that sounds quirky until you actually go, and then you wonder why you waited. More than 100 local bands play on actual neighborhood porches across one of Atlanta's best walkable neighborhoods, all day from 11am to 6pm. You wander around, stumble onto good music, grab food from vendors, and just enjoy the city doing its thing. Tickets start at $10. It happens once a year. That's all you need to know.
scoring_notes: Annual event with a distinctly Atlanta flavor and a format that is genuinely unlike anything else on the calendar. Appeals to the culturally curious, established-adult segment of East Cobb. The drive into the city is a mild deterrent for some readers, but the low ticket price and unique format make it worth flagging. Better fit for the empty-nester or young-professional adjacent segment than for families with young kids.

price: From $49 per day, $99 for 3-day pass; Family 3-day from $99
blurb: If you have a teenager in the house who games, this one is for them, and honestly it might be for you too. DreamHack Atlanta is one of the biggest gaming and esports festivals in the world, and it is landing at the Georgia World Congress Center this weekend for three days. We're talking live Call of Duty League competition, Counter-Strike 2 tournaments, open gaming floors, cosplay, creator meetups, and a full expo. This is not a small local LAN party. It draws tens of thousands of people from across the country. Family 3-day tickets start at $99 for up to 2 adults and 6 kids, which is a solid deal for a full weekend of entertainment. If your kid has been into gaming at all, this is the kind of experience they will talk about for a while.
scoring_notes: East Cobb skews toward families with teens and college-age kids, which is the core DreamHack demographic. Parents in this income bracket will not blink at the ticket price. High uniqueness because this is a major international event that does not happen every weekend. The adult audience with no kids at home may not self-select for this one, but the teen and young adult angle is a strong hook for the newsletter's family segment.

event_name: Sherri Shepherd: Make It Make Sense Tour
blurb: Sherri Shepherd is bringing her stand-up tour to the Buckhead Theatre this Saturday, and if you watch The View or know her from anything else she has done, you already know what you are getting. Sharp, unfiltered, funny, and very much for grown-ups. This is a solid girls' night out or a date-night pick for anyone who wants to laugh without having to explain the jokes to their kids. Buckhead Theatre is a great room. Get tickets before they're gone.
scoring_notes: Emmy-winning TV personality with broad name recognition among East Cobb's 40-plus demographic. Adult skew works perfectly for the empty-nester segment. One-night-only touring show gives it real urgency. Buckhead Theatre is an accessible, comfortable venue for this audience. Comedy is broadly appealing and low-barrier.

blurb: Marcus King is one of the best live acts in the country right now, and he is bringing his full band to the Fox Theatre on Saturday night. He plays a mix of Southern rock, soul, and blues that does not fit neatly into any one category, which is exactly what makes him worth seeing. The Fox is one of the great rooms in Atlanta, and a show like this is the kind of night out you plan a babysitter around. If you have been on the fence about seeing him, this is a great excuse to finally go.
scoring_notes: Grammy-nominated artist at an iconic venue is a strong pull for East Cobb's educated, culturally engaged, higher-income adults and empty nesters. The 42 median age lines up well with Marcus King's core fanbase. A proper night-out event, not something the reader can do any weekend. Strong uniqueness because touring shows like this come through once and then they're gone.

venue: Holy Transfiguration Greek Orthodox Church, Marietta
blurb: The Marietta Greek Festival is back this weekend, and it is one of the best reasons to leave the house all month. Think authentic Greek food, fresh pastries, cold drinks, live music, and traditional dancing, all in one spot just minutes from home. This is the kind of event you can do with the whole family on Saturday afternoon or turn into a proper Friday night out. It runs all three days, so you can pick whatever time works. Get there before Sunday, when things wrap up at 4pm. Full details at mariettagreekfestival.com.
scoring_notes: Annual event but a genuine community tradition with deep local roots, right in East Cobb's backyard. Broad appeal across the mix of established families and empty nesters. Flexible scheduling across three days makes it easy for any household to attend. Food, music, and culture combo is a reliable crowd-pleaser for this demographic. Slight uniqueness deduction because it recurs annually, but it only happens once a year so it still earns a strong score.
